live_fields.value: THREE LIVE matters, refreshed to 2026-06 state (the dossier's facts.md sec.9 / M5 framing was the FY2025-10-K snapshot and is now stale on all three): (1) DOJ SEARCH (US v. Google, Judge Mehta, DDC): remedies were FINALIZED 2025-09-02 -- Google KEEPS Chrome and Android (court REJECTED the DOJ's structural divestiture); remedies are behavioral (no exclusive default-distribution contracts for Search/Chrome/Assistant/Gemini + must share search index & user-interaction data with qualified rivals). Google filed Notice of Appeal 2026-01-16; DOJ cross-appealed 2026-02-03 seeking divestiture; Google's opening DC-Circuit brief filed 2026-05-22; oral argument expected late-2026/early-2027, final resolution possibly 2028. NET: the larger search case resolved WITHOUT a breakup -- materially LESS severe than a structural remedy, and the dossier never recorded this outcome. (2) DOJ ADTECH (US v. Google, Judge Brinkema, EDVA): liability found 2025-04 (illegal monopoly in publisher ad server + ad exchange). The REMEDY trial has now CONCLUDED -- closing arguments delivered; DOJ seeks divestiture of AdX (then DFP) + open-sourcing of auction logic, but Judge Brinkema voiced SKEPTICISM toward structural remedies and concern over divestiture timing vs behavioral fixes. Ruling pending (no order yet as of 2026-06). The dossier's 'structural remedy pending' is directionally right but understates that the remedy phase is now post-trial with the judge leaning away from a forced breakup. (3) EC ADTECH FINE: the dossier says 'EUR 3.0B charge appealed Nov 2025'; the FORMAL EC decision landed 2025-09-05 = EUR 2.95B (~USD 3.45B) fine + cease-and-desist on adtech self-preferencing (4th EU penalty); Google is appealing. The ~$3.5B FY2025 P&L charge (claim_ledger GOOG.A1.2025K.026) is the accrual for this and remains a correct FILED number.

2026 capex guidance $180-190B (midpoint $185B), with 2027 capex to 'significantly increase' vs 2026 (per the June 2026 424B5 financing document, GOOGL-424B5-2026-06.009). No new quantitative guidance has superseded this as of as_of; next major catalyst is Q2 2026 earnings (~late July 2026). This is the FILED forward-capex signal that drives the owner-earnings (maintenance-vs-growth capex split) open question; unchanged this refresh.
